front: Poetry: collection divided into 'Objects,' 'Food,' and 'Rooms'; includes 'A Carafe, That Is a Blind Glass' and 'Glazed Glitter'
Novel: the narrator imagines writing a book titled 'The Wives of Geniuses I Have Sat With' and describes a poodle named Basket
back: The Autobiography of Alice B. Toklas (Gertrude Stein)
work: The Autobiography of Alice B. Toklas

front: Novel: opens with a parable of a man yelling 'Stop! I did not drag my father beyond this tree' as he is dragged through an orchard
back: The Autobiography of Alice B. Toklas (Gertrude Stein)
work: The Autobiography of Alice B. Toklas

front: Novel: set in the fictional Bridgepoint (based on Baltimore); 'The Good Anna,' 'Melanctha,' and 'The Gentle Lena'

Novel: the longest story follows a mixed-race woman who dies alone after a series of love affairs; James Weldon Johnson praised it as the first white-authored love story treating Black people normally
Poem: repeats 'shutters shut and open so do queens' and ends with 'Let me recite what history teaches. History teaches.'
back: If I Told Him: A Completed Portrait of Picasso (Gertrude Stein)

front: Poem: the author's lover forced a change of 'may' to 'can' throughout due to jealousy over an ex named May Bookstaver; includes the single-line stanza 'I wish that I had spoken only of it all'
Opera: Saint Ignatius sees 'pigeons on the grass alas'; collaboration with Virgil Thomson
back: Four Saints in Three Acts (Gertrude Stein / Virgil Thomson)

front: Opera: about Susan B. Anthony; ends with a ghostly voice declaiming 'my long life, my long life' after a statue is unveiled at the Capitol
back: The Mother of Us All (Gertrude Stein / Virgil Thomson)

front: Author: said of her hometown Oakland that 'there is no there there,' which inspired the title of Tommy Orange's novel There There
Author: sat over 80-90 times for an angular portrait finished from memory after the artist studied Iberian sculpture; the artist said 'she will' come to resemble it
back: Portrait of Gertrude Stein by Pablo Picasso

front: Author: Mina Loy wrote she worked in the 'laboratory of vocabulary' to 'extract a radium of the word'
Author: a 'poster portrait' by Charles Demuth features a mask of this person hovering over '1 2 3' and 'Love Love Love'
Novel: thousand-page work about the Hersland and Dehning families exemplifying the author's technique of repeating everyday words
Author: her essay 'Miss Furr and Miss Skeene' may be the first literary work to use 'gay' for a same-sex relationship
